opps.title: NIH NICHD OIT Application and Website Support Services
opps.description:
The NICHD Office of Information Technology  requires continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance, Section 508 Compliance, database and data mart support, application infrastructure support, and other technical services including but not limited to the support of new and emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine learning (ML)/Robotic Process Automation (RPA), NICHD and/or NIH Wide. The focus of these IT support activities is creation, development, maintenance, operation, and support of a web application infrastructure that provides an efficient, effective, reliable, and secure computing environment in support of NICHD's and NIH's mission and supports staff in the efficient and effective utilization of these capabilities. 

Details on the evaluation criteria are contained in the attached RFQ.

In order to be considered for award, a vendor will be required to cover the SIN(s) listed below awarded on their Schedule contract. Any quote received from a contractor that does not cover the required SIN(s) will not be further evaluated and subsequently ineligible for award. Required SIN(s): 	54151S (Information Technology Professional Services); 541611 (Management and Financial Consulting, Acquisition and Grants Management Support, and Business Program and Project Management Services)
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institutes of Health (NIH), specifically the Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD), is seeking continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance. The goal of this contract is to ensure Section 508 Compliance, provide database and data mart support, and offer application infrastructure support, including the integration of new technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML). 

This initiative aims to create a reliable and secure computing envir

opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

opps.title: Power Platform Development Support for Office of Emergency Management
opps.description:
The Department of the Interior (DOI), Interior Business Center (IBC), Acquisition Services Directorate (AQD), is conducting market research on behalf of the Office of Emergency Management (OEM). 

OEM relies on Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint, PowerApps, Power Automate, and Dataverse for mission-critical emergency management operations including incident reporting, resource tracking, workflow automation, and continuity of operations support. AQD is seeking information from qualified vendors capable of supporting the development, modernization, and sustainment of these solutions in a federal environment.

Vendors with demonstrated experience in Power Platform development, Application Lifecycle Management (ALM), Microsoft Graph API integration, Section 508 compliance, and federal IT security standards are encouraged to respond.

Please review ALL attached documents before responding, including the Request for Information (RFI) and the DOI Information Technology Baseline Compliance Contract Guidelines. 

All submission requirements, capability areas of interest, and response instructions are detailed in the attached RFI.

Responses are due by July 9, 2026 11:00 AM Eastern.

All responses must be submitted in PDF format via email to Candy_Mott-Harris@ibc.doi.gov with the subject line: RFI Response OEM Power Platform Development Support.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.description:
***03/05/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/31/2026, estimated period of performance start date updated to 09/16/2026, and anticipated award date updated to 09/01/2026.***
***01/13/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/02/2026.***

***9/24/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 09/11/2025 to 11/03/2025; Dollar Range updated from $10M-$20M to $5M-$10M.***

USCIS has a requirement for Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS).  USCIS seeks to establish a more sustainable and effective continuous monitoring program, leveraging data from security and governance tools to enhance our ability to identify and mitigate cybersecurity risks, evaluate emerging technologies, streamline security-related tasks. Additionally, we seek to transform the Ongoing Authorization (OA) program to incorporate new capabilities and risk models for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and implement appropriate governance measures. The CMASS resources will continue to support security authorization and continuous monitoring activities, the Vulnerability Management & Configuration Management Programs, role-based cybersecurity training, and system stakeholder outreach to ensure USCIS meets security and DHS Information System Performance Plan (ISPP) requirements.

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.description:
The U.S. Fire Administration (USFA), located at 16825 S. Seton Avenue, Emmitsburg, Maryland, requires visual arts support to provide professional graphic design services for USFA and NDEMU staff. The graphic and multimedia design staff will be located onsite at USFA.

Design staff will create visual assets that highlight and elevate USFAâ€™s brand, reinforcing its position as a trusted leader in fire and emergency medical services. Visual arts support services are expected to recommend best design practices.

The government will provide all necessary graphic design software and material supplies.

Design staff responsibilities include:

Publication layout and digital formatting
Training materials
Factsheets, handouts, flyers, and infographics
Illustrations
3D and 2D animations and simulations
Animated and motion graphics video productions
Program graphics and posters
Production work (large format printing, laminating, etc.)
Limited studio photography

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open Solicitation
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

************************************************************************************

opps.title: NOS Cloud Migration & Data Infrastructure Support
opps.description: Comprehensive data management support to develop and implement a modern, interoperable ocean data infrastructure that will facilitate cloud migration, simplify data management, and accelerate AI integration across the organization. The core objective of this contract is to transition from traditional data architectures to a unifying multi-cloud environment, establishing governance standards and foundational support for AI development, including designing for the MLOps Lifecycle and simplifying AI access. This initiative is crucial for advancing NOS’s cloud-first strategy, supporting projects like marine critical minerals, and ensuring functional management of data and metadata. Potential vendors should structure their proposals to provide expertise in infrastructure engineering, data governance, and cloud architecture, covering positions such as Cloud Architect, Data Center Administrator, Data Technician, and IT Engineer.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)

opps.title: BSEE Data Management Services - Oil and Gas Experience
opps.description:
BSEE Data Management Services - Gulf of America and Pacific Regions Oil and Gas Experience. 

The Department of the Interior (DOI), Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) is issuing this competitive Request for Quote (RFQ) to eligible General Services Administration (GSA) Federal Supply Schedule (FSS) Contract holders under GSA Schedule  54151S, "Information Technology Professional Services IT Professional Services and/or labor categories for database planning and design; systems analysis, integration, and design; programming, conversion and implementation support; network services, data/records management, and testing" for the purpose of entering into a Task Order for BSEE Data Management Services.  

The objective of the task order is to provide data and information management support services to the BSEE Gulf of America Region's various program offices (primary site), the BSEE Pacific Regions' program offices (secondary site).  The Contractor will provide high, mid, and entry level data and information management expertise to support data collection, analysis, imaging, and retrieval/delivery services.  Other services will include project management, technical consulting, and outreach services.  The requirements will encompass such functions as data entry, data loading, document processing, document indexing, document scanning, data validation, data quality assurance, data archiving, and overseeing projects.  

See attached RFQ 140E0126Q0028.

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) Total Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ Total Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

***********************************************************************************

opps.title: Decennial Transformation & Application Modernization Acquisition
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework supports the Business Ecosystem, the Decennial (2030 Census and early 2040 Census research), as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, research methodology programs, and other program areas within USCB. The purpose is to plan, develop, document, test, train, monitor, manage, and maintain software architecture and applications to support the 2030 Decennial Census operations. DTAM is the modernization of existing applications and the development of new applications, across Decennial systems, that will be leveraged for the Decennial Census Programs, in support of the 2028 Census Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Census Bureau's massive digital modernization initiative — the CenTAM framework — covering IT systems for the 2030 Decennial Census. Includes application development, legacy modernization, cloud migration, data collection platforms, and systems to support economic, geographic, and demographic programs. Multiple separate contract awards expected under this framework.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has direct Census past performance ($4.5M MAF/TIGER modernization, $4.5M EDL Applications, $2.8M NOAA app mod). Census is also BLN24's highest-value client ($18.4M). This is a real opportunity — but it's a large technology modernization vehicle, not comms or HCD. The question is which specific task areas under CenTAM align with what Clarity24 can actually deliver vs. what larger primes will own. Full & Open means big integrators will compete.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Get into the weeds on the CenTAM acquisition structure — it's a collection of complementary contracts, not one big award. Find which specific package maps to digital modernization vs. IT infrastructure vs. data engineering. Clarity24 should be positioned for the digital/app mod pieces. Due 2027 — shape now.

This acquisition is set-aside for small business concerns. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov.

This solicitation is 100% set-aside for small business under the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code 541810, with a small business size standard of $25,500,000M. Product Service Code (PSC) is R701. The contracting officer reserves the right to award under a different NAICS if the company would qualify as a small business under a different NAICS.

a)	Line Item 0001 - Illinois High School Association (IHSA) Advertising 
b)	Line Item 0002 - Southern Illinois University (SIU) Advertising

This is a non-personal services contract to provide advertising services for the Illinois Air National Guard (ILANG). The contractor shall provide all personnel, equipment, supplies, facilities, transportation, tools, materials, supervision, and non-personal services necessary to perform this advertising venture as defined in the Performance Work Statement (PWS) except for those items specified as government furnished property and services. opps.title: Recompete OMD's (formerly MDL) technical services contract.
opps.description: Technical services contract for Model and Application Development support, including NBM, NWSConnect, Aviation model development, Storm Surge Model development, Warn on Forecast System (WoFS) development and Data dissemination services. Product development areas include: statistical modeling, digital database development; model interpretation, verification and evaluation; cloud infrastructure development and support; storm surge prediction; Virtual Lab infrastructure development and support, and decision support application development (NWS Connect). The goal of this modeling effort is to develop an accurate enough model guidance suite to allow it to reduce or eliminate the need for forecaster intervention, allowing the shift of focus of our limited forecast staff to support core partner decisions that have a greater impact on the mission to protect life and property.

opps.title: Homeland Security Infrastructure Security (HSIN) Critical Infrastructure Information Sharing Environment Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires program support services for the Homeland Security Information Network - Critical Infrastructure Information Sharing Environment. Homeland Security Information Network - Critical Infrastructure (HSIN-CI) is Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency's (CISA's) largest Site Collection operating under the HSIN Enterprise System, which is maintained by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) HSIN Program Management Office (HSIN-PMO) located within the DHS Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO). The Contractor shall provide support for HSIN-CI and Critical Infrastructure- Information Sharing Environment (CI-ISE) to include: program management, stakeholder engagement, platform management, technical assistance for users and HSIN-CI Program office staff, site design / SharePoint design and architecture and maintenance support, along with technical project management, technical consulting, facilitation and documentation support to DHS through the enterprise architecture and lifecycle sustainment of the HSIN-CI portals. Work will be performed at a combination of the Contractor's facilities and the Department of Homeland Security at 4200 Wilson Boulevard, Arlington, Virginia.

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material

opps.title: Enterprise Skills Assessment Platform - Sources Sought
opps.description:
SOURCES SOUGHT FOR ENTERPRISE SKILLS ASSESSMENT PLATFORM

In accordance with FAR Part 10 – Market Research, this sources sought notice is issued by the United States Army Special Operations Command (USASOC) for planning purposes only, to determine industry interest and capability in providing a commercially available Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution for systematic skills assessment and upskilling of the technical workforce. This notice does not constitute a Request for Proposal. The Government is not seeking or accepting unsolicited proposals. This notice should not be construed as a contract, a promise to contract, or a commitment of any kind by the Government. Requests for solicitation will not receive a response. All information is to be submitted at no cost or obligation to the U.S. Government. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this announcement or any follow-up information requests. The U.S. Government reserves the right to reject, in whole or in part, any input as a result of this market survey. The U.S. Government is not obligated to notify respondents of the results of this survey. If a formal solicitation is generated at a later date, a solicitation notice will be published.

The Department of the Army is conducting market research to identify potential sources capable of providing direct labor, materials, technical support, and administrative services necessary for the provisioning and operation. The platform must support user onboarding, role-based skills assessment, data analytics, and ongoing technical support for up to 500 users. The primary objective is to enhance mission readiness by identifying and closing skill gaps in critical domains such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Data Engineering, Cybersecurity, and Software Development. The estimated period of performance (PoP) is from 15 July 2026 to 14 July 2027.

Attached is a working copy of the Performance Work Statement (PWS)

1.0 INTRODUCTION & BACKGROUND
This is a Request for Information (RFI) and Sources Sought notice issued solely for information and planning purposes; it does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. In accordance with 15.201(e), responses to this RFI are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  This notice is issued solely for market research and planning purposes, and the Government will not obligate any funds or reimburse respondents for any costs associated with preparing their submissions.

The United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program requires robust, secure, and highly analytical database services to support the tracking, management, and mitigation of corrosion across the enterprise. The CPAC program is critical to extending the lifecycle, ensuring the safety, and maintaining the combat readiness of USMC Ground Equipment.

The Government is seeking to identify qualified sources capable of providing specialized database management and analytical services, specifically those with demonstrated past performance working with USMC corrosion data and ground equipment platforms.

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: Management and Governance Directorate (MGD) Financial Analytics, Governance, and Acquisition Support
opps.description:
This re-compete acquisition seeks comprehensive professional support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Office of Information Technology (OIT)'s Management and Governance Directorate (MGD), Financial Management Acquisition Division (FMAD), and Strategic Planning & Mission Engagement Division (SPME). Services encompass strategy implementation, financial planning, and investment management, aiming to enhance OIT's budget formulation, planning, and reporting, and ensure transparent, data-driven financial decision-making. Key support areas include Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC) coordination, strategic financial planning, strategy management, and critical support for the Unified Immigration Portal (UIP) program, focusing on cost models and reporting. The requirement also involves extensive analytics, automation, data visualization, and platforms technical services, including the development and maintenance of executive dashboards (e.g., Power BI, Power Platform, Apptio Cloud ability). These services are essential for managing extensive financial resources and delivering mission-critical IT capabilities that support trade administration, traveler movement, and border security.

 Acquisition Strategy: 100% Small Business Set-Aside competed under General Services Administration (GSA) OASIS+ Small Business (SB) Pool.

opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description: This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP)systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies. Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.title: USCG CGLO PROGRAM ANALYST
opps.description:
U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) USCG Liaison Office, situated onboard Naval Air Station Pensacola, FL at 211 South Avenue is seeking a Program/Management Analyst to fulfill its operational needs. This is a Firm-Fixed Price order.

The requirements document in the form of a Statement of Work is attached.

Detailed information regarding the incumbent award can be accessed on https://www.usaspending.gov/, under Award ID: 70Z04325FSMTC0005.

This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotations are being requested.
Evaluation: A Firm-Fixed purchase order will be made to the contractor that represents the best value. Quotes will be evaluated based on technical approach, experience of key personnel, and price. Under the 'Experience of Key Personnel' factor, the government will consider the experience of the proposed key person and the estimated time required to train them on government-specific systems and procedures. Proposals that demonstrate the key person has relevant experience and requires minimal training will be viewed more favorably.

The anticipated award date is 10 days after solicitation closing date.

All quoters must submit the following: Submission of a quote that does not contain all items requested below may result in elimination from consideration of award. Offerors must submit each item of quote in a separate electronic attachment clearly identified as described below. Offerors are responsible for including sufficient details to permit a complete and accurate evaluation of each quote.

DISCUSSIONS: The Government intends to award without discussions but reserves the right to conduct discussion should it be deemed in the Government's best interest.

VOLUME 1:
Section A Total price will be evaluated by the Government. Proposed price should reflect a clear understanding of the requirements, is consistent with the various elements of the offer.  

VOLUME 2 
Section A Technical Proposal: Offeror shall submit company's detailed technical approach and method

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source - Quorum Analytics
opps.agency: DHS Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Quorum Analytics, LLC, 1 Thomas Circle, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, DC 20005.

 Background

To fulfill its mission, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) requires access to specialized scientific journals and databases to stay current with emerging research and developments. Quorum is a leading platform for federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and a legislative engagement platform. By acquiring Quorum, DHS S&T staff will gain real-time access to government data, monitor legislative activity, and conduct comprehensive policy analysis. This capability will strengthen operational effectiveness, support informed decision-making, and streamline research efforts, ultimately advancing mission-critical objectives. S&T will procure five (5)  Quorum subscriptions for (5) users.

Quorum is the sole provider of an integrated platform that offers real-time federal bill tracking, social media monitoring, and legislative engagement capabilities. No other vendor delivers this unique combination of services with the same breadth, depth, and real-time functionality.

Quorum is uniquely positioned to meet DHS S&T’s needs for the following reasons:

Exclusive Integrated Platform: Quorum is the only platform that combines federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and legislative engagement in a single, unified system. This integration is not available from any other vendor.

Real-Time Access: Quorum provides

opps.title: HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES



HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot



June 26, 2026



1. Purpose

The purpose of this acquisition is to obtain short-duration, firm-fixed-price pilot awards that function as inclusive, all-you-can-eat-style access bundles, within a stated usage envelope, for up to [1,000] authorized portable HHS power users per resultant award, with potential priced options to increase the authorized portable-user quantity up to 10,000. The objective is to let power users exercise advanced models, advanced features, integration paths, native apps, reporting functions, administrative controls, and security-boundary options in a forward-leaning enterprise environment before HHS finalizes its broader enterprise AI solution.



The primary purpose of the advanced power-user pilot is to establish operational baselines for emerging and frontier AI capabilities  to enable HHS to accurately forecast enterprise demand, define enterprise operating models, establish governance requirements, determine enterprise scalability, or develop future pricing structures.



HHS also intends to mature enterprise AI governance, attribution, allocation, and consumption measurement capabilities throughout the pilot period, including support for capability-level attribution, program-level allocation, enterprise reporting, and AI Consumption Unit (ACU) normalization methodologies.



The pilot shall enable the Government to baseline actual power-user usage, determine enterprise-feasible operational methodology, identify and roadmap which models and features require configuration or customization, identify levels and timing of customization, establish security and authorization logic, and formulate a common enterprise logical and operational model for AI use at HHS.



HHS requires the pilot to establish a practical operating model for commercial-parity access to advanced AI models and features in a forward-leaning enterprise environment. The contractor shall suppo

opps.title: Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE)
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot).  Assisting in planning and executing the rollout of Copilot /AI service in accordance with DHS and USCIS security and privacy policies as well as additional Microsoft 365 Services, USCIS may choose to adopt.   Additionally, USCIS needs support for M365 service integrations with both Microsoft and non-Microsoft Technologies, e.g., AirWatch, ServiceNow, Archive360, JAMF, and AgilQuest.

*Small business set aside to be determined through market research. USCIS intends to issue a Request for Information during May 2026.

opps.title: Software Development, Operations, Maintenance (O&M) Support Services
opps.description:
The mission of the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) is to improve health 
and achieve health equity through access to quality services, a skilled health workforce and 
innovative programs. HRSA assures the availability of quality health care to low income, 
uninsured, isolated, vulnerable, and special needs populations. As the world's leading public 
health agency, HRSA has the responsibility for promoting health and quality of life by 
preventing and controlling disease, injury and disability. HRSA works with States, local public 
health agencies and partners throughout the Nation and the world to accomplish this mission. 
HRSA serves as the national focus for developing and applying disease prevention and control 
methodologies, environmental health strategies, and health promotion and education activities 
designed to improve the health of the people of the United States and throughout the world. 
The contractor shall be capable of providing Software Development, Operations, Maintenance 
(O&M) support services for Custom, Enterprise Applications and Cloud Platform Systems as 
HRSA functions evolve or are incorporated into broader HHS enterprise or operating models, 
without interruption to services or degradation of security posture.

opps.title: Bioinformatics Analysis of Livestock Genomics
opps.agency: FDA Office of Acquisition and Grant Services
opps.description:
CVM possesses significant bioinformatics expertise; however, personnel with the specific skills to perform these tasks are currently allocated to other high-priority agency projects. To meet the urgent timelines for this analysis, CVM requires external, focused support to perform the necessary visual validation of swine variants from an already generated list of candidate variants and the generation of CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This approach will allow the project to proceed efficiently while enabling CVM's internal experts to provide oversight and ensure the validated variants and CRISPRme input files meet their precise scientific requirements. 

Please see attached RFQ.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Center for Veterinary Medicine (CVM) at the FDA is seeking external support for bioinformatics analysis due to personnel being allocated to other high-priority projects. 

 The goal is to validate genomic variants in CRISPR/Cas9 gene-edited swine samples and generate CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This will allow CVM's internal experts to oversee the project while ensuring that the scientific requirements are met efficiently.

Work Details
 The contractor will perform the following tasks: 
1) Visual validation of genomic variants in whole genome sequencing

opps.title: Destruction Cart
opps.description:
AMENDMENT 0001:

The purpose of this amendment is to extend the submission of quotes deadline for the subject solicitation.  The purpose of extending the deadline is to allow the multi-functional team (MFT) time to review and provide a response to questions submitted by vendors received prior to the question submission deadline dated on Tuesday, 23 June 2026 at 11:00 AM(CDT. Please refer to the new attachment "4. Attachment - Amendment 0001, FA301626Q0139, Destruction Cart, Extend Quote Deadline" for additional details and require vendors to SIGNED & ACKNOWLEDGED the AMENDMENT. 

****************************************************************************************************************************************************

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products prepared in accordance with RFO part 12. This announcement constitutes only solicitation. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.


	Solicitation number FA301626Q0139 is issued as a request for quotation (RFQ) for Purchase of Destruction Cart for the Joint Electromagnetic Warfare Center (JWEC) at Joint Base San Antonio (JBSA).
	The Governement shall solicit 100% Total Small business set aside under the assigned NAICS code.


Description of Product: The purpose of this statement of need is for a self-contained mobile, and secure data destruction system. The primary objective is to acquire a comprehensive solution capable of completely sanitizingand physically destroying both magnetic (rotational) and solid-state hard drives in accordance with National Security Agency (NSA) standards for handling Top Secret data. This capability is critical for ensuring compliance with data security protocols and mitigating the risk of data spillage. The system must provide a complete, verifiable audit trail of the erasure and destruction process for accountability and reporting purposes.

Work Details

The selected service provider must deliver the highest standard of professional support to meet the operational volume and service needs of UNICOR sites. See attached SOW for details.
This Request for Information (RFI) does not create any representation, either express or implied, that if a procurement process does proceed, that the participant's will be given any preference or special consideration.

opps.title: National Incident Management System (NIMS) Implementation, Resource Typing, National Qualification System (NQS) Development, and Administrative Support.
opps.description: The work to be completed under this contract advances the implementation of the National Preparedness System (NPS) and NIMS, to include: 1) developing materials to support NIMS implementation, 2) developing and producing new or updated NIMS resource typing documents, 3) supporting the development of the NQS, and 4) developing and implementing a strategy to collect and analyze preparedness data across National Integration Center (NIC) program areas. These four focus areas are central to the nation's ability to build, sustain, and deliver the core capabilities in support of the National Preparedness Goal and System. The NIMS resource typing and NQS documents are essential to ensuring the maximum level of mission readiness at the local, state, tribal, territorial, and federal levels for day-to-day mutual aid and preparedness for major disasters.  The contractor shall provide the NIC with technical, administrative, and programmatic support services, as specified in the Performance Work Statement (PWS).

opps.title: Agile Development Services II (ADS2)
opps.description:
Provide skilled information technology labor services to the Coast Guard CG-C5I-Engineering and C5I Service Center Business Operations Division for Agile application development, database maintenance, and custom decision-making tool implementation to improve and modernize technology service delivery to operational Coast Guard units.

Business Governance and Continuous Process Improvement
- Assess strategic landscapes/events impacting Coast Guard C5I service delivery to define, establish, and update long-term strategic objectives.
- Conduct research and analysis for strategic growth planning and execution guidance.
- Engage organizational stakeholders to plan, support and facilitate meetings, determine solutions to stakeholder problems and develop Courses of Action (COA) options to achieve organization goals. 
- Provide guidance and execution support in defining and measuring effectiveness of governance efforts and assets, and in continually improving governance effectiveness over time.
- Develop and support Continual Process Improvement (CPI) initiatives by tracking progress, measuring success, and providing training products to support these efforts (for example: Lean Six Sigma White and Yellow Belt training).
- Support and provide guidance adopting maturity models, including conducting organizational analyses to research, develop, and execute action plans detailing steps and progress adopting maturity models and steps organizational components should take to mature capabilities in alignment with maturity models.
- Recommend, develop, and assist in implementing strategies and action plans that optimize adoption and effective organizational change management (OCM).

Business Performance
- Assist in developing, implementing, and routinely updating performance measurement systems, metrics frameworks, and toolsets that deliver capabilities that monitor, control, visualize, and maintain organizational functionality.
- Perform data Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) procedures

rex_analysis.what_they_need: NOAA's vehicle for Science, Engineering, and Technical Support Services across its research offices — weather, oceans, fisheries, atmospheric research. Task orders span scientific program support, systems engineering, data management, IT systems, and environmental compliance across ~235 NOAA facilities.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has $7M in confirmed NOAA delivery (cloud migration, app modernization), which is real past performance. But SETSS is primarily a science and engineering vehicle — meteorology, oceanography, fisheries, facilities. BLN24's lanes (HCD, comms, data analytics) are narrow within this IDIQ. Best path is monitoring for specific task orders in digital/data/comms scope rather than pursuing the IDIQ prime. Due Jan 2027 — early awareness only.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Watch for task order RFPs under the existing SETSS vehicle that align with digital, UX, or data analytics scope. Clarity24's NOAA relationship is the asset here. Avoid chasing the IDIQ prime unless a teaming partner with deep NOAA scientific credentials is in place.

opps.title: 6515--SteriDate Kiosk System with Accompanying Accessories
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for Brand Name or Equal to Steridate Kiosk System with accompanying accessories in accordance with the Statement of Work and Salient Characteristics as prepared in accordance with the format in Subpart 12.201-1(c)(2), as supplemented in the attached Request for Quotes (RFQ). A firm-fixed price purchase order is anticipated. Simplified Acquisition Procedures IAW FAR 12 will be used. This announcement constitutes only solicitation.
The solicitation number is 36C26026Q0781 and is attached.
The solicitation document and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through:
Federal Acquisition Circular: FAC - 2025-06 Effective Date: August 2025.
This solicitation is set aside for Small Businesses and the associated NAICS Code 541519 has a small business size standard of $34 Million. 
Award will be based upon a comparative evaluation of quotes in accordance with the Simplified Acquisition Procedures of FAR 12. Comparative evaluation is the side-by-side comparison of quotes based on factors resulting in a Contracting Officer decision for the quote most favorable to the Government. The award will be made to the response most advantageous to the Government. Responses should contain your best terms and conditions.
Quotes must be emailed to: jeffrey.kelley1@va.gov and received no later than 1300 PST on 07/16/2025. Quotes may be submitted on the attached RFQ or the vendor s own form. All questions must be submitted in writing and will not be answered via telephone, no exceptions.
No late quotations will be accepted.
See attached document: RFQ - 36C26026Q0781.
opps.summary:
Background
 This solicitation is issued by the Department of Veterans Affairs, specifically the Portland VA Medical Center, as a combined synopsis/solicitation for a SteriDate Kiosk System with accompanying accessories. The goal of this contract is to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of sterilization tracking within the facility. The procurement aims to implement reliable and durable labeling solutions for autoclave environments and ensure seamless integration of hardware and software components to optimize workflow.

Work Details
 The contract involves the procurement of the following item

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

opps.title: Telecom Service Data
opps.agency: DOL Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration and Management
opps.description:
The purpose of this RFI is to gather information about potential vendors to obtain current monthly wireless and wireline telecommunications service plan pricing.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) has an ongoing interest in market data with which to analyze, evaluate, and improve the indexes it produces. Specifically, the BLS is seeking detailed data for one year from a survey asking households to record information about their purchases for telecommunications services. BLS would like to wholly purchase this data, as opposed to a temporary lease of the data.

The BLS is the principle Federal agency responsible for measuring price changes in the economy and constructs indexes using data collected from both households and business establishments. Because of the importance of BLS data, it is critical that the component indexes are accurate, timely, and relevant.
opps.summary:

 The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) is the principal Federal agency responsible for measuring price changes in the economy. The BLS aims to gather information about potential vendors to obtain current monthly wireless and wireline telecommunications service plan pricing. Specifically, BLS seeks detailed data from a survey asking households to record their purchases for telecommunications services, intending to wholly purchase this data rather than lease it temporarily.

opps.title: GSA - Promoting the Administration's Domestic Sourcing/Buy American Goals - MRAS
opps.description:
GSA Wants Your Feedback to Enhance Domestic Preference in GSA Acquisition Programs - Market Research

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY

(NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the link into a new browser.)

Additional Information:
Your company may receive multiple RFI notifications for this requirement since it is being posted on GSA eBuy, SAM.gov. and marketed widely on GSA platforms. Your company only needs to complete the RFI survey one time.

Any questions regarding this RFI can be sent to: rfi@research.gsa.gov. Please include the RFI number in your email title.

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY 

(NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the link into a new browser.)

Additional Information:
Your company may receive multiple RFI notifications for this requirement since it is being posted on GSA eBuy, SAM.gov. and marketed widely on GSA platforms.  Your company only needs to complete the RFI survey one time.

Any questions regarding this RFI can be sent to: rfi@research.gsa.gov.  Please include the RFI number in your email title.

opps.title: NIJ-NamUs
opps.description:
National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ).
The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to: (1) operate without interruption, (2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and (3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.
The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. 

The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.  Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor.  The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them.
Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---

THIS IS A SOURCES SOUGHT ANNOUNCEMENT ONLY. This is NOT a solicitation, but market research to determine potential sources to Omnissa Horizon Software Support on Keesler AFB, MS as described in the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS).
This notice does not constitute a promise to issue a solicitation in the future. Further, we are not at this time seeking proposals; therefore, DO NOT request a copy of a solicitation or submit a proposal as a result of this notice. Responders are advised the Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this sources sought and that all submissions become Government property and will not be returned. All costs associated with responding to this sources sought will be solely at the responding party's expense. 
Not responding to this sources sought does not preclude participation in any future solicitation, should one be issued. Responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. No basis for claim against the Government shall arise from a response to the notice. It is the responsibility of the interested parties to monitor the SAM.gov website for additional information pertaining to this sources sought. 
RESPONSE INFORMATION: 
Responses to this Sources Sought Notice must be received no later than 12:00 PM CST, on Tuesday, 30 June 2026. Responses should be emailed to: joshua.brown.138@us.af.mil and priscilla.brown.5@us.af.mil. It is the responsibility of the respondent to ensure their email has been received by contacting either the Contract Specialist, SSgt Joshua Brown, at 228-377-1808 or Contracting Officer, Priscilla Brown, at 228-377-1827 or via email PRIOR to the deadline. Responses and/or inquiries WILL NOT be accepted past the deadline. 
Interested parties should include their company name, address, phone number, point of contact, CAGE code, company size status, and whether or not they are a U.S. or foreign-owned firm. All

opps.title: Database Migration to Open-Source (DMO) Contract
opps.description: The purpose of this Economic Applications Division (EAD) Database Migration to Open-Source (DMO) procurement is to ensure that the Economic Directorate (ECON) is sufficiently resourced to meet the critical needs of its mission. The Contractor shall furnish the necessary personnel and services to perform the Performance-Based Work Statement (PWS) tasks.  This TO covers Small and Large Database Migration Support, Benchmarking, Optimization, and Performance Tuning as well as Migration Metrics, Code Conversion, Database Queries, and Report Generation, while leveraging existing technology that improves services and provides better customer service experiences.  The DMO TO will also provide software and resources needed to assist mission needs related to Economic Survey Operations across the Economic Census and Current Survey areas, respectively.   ECON / EAD is currently seeking an end-to-end data migration accelerator and architectural

opps.title: Spectrum Valuation
opps.description: NTIA seeks contractor support for spectrum valuation to design and deliver a defensible, repeatable, and data driven valuation of designated radio-frequency spectrum bands to inform strategic planning, budgeting, policy development, and potential reallocation/sharing decisions. The effort will produce an analytical framework, an economic model, market analysis, operational utility assessment, and valuation outputs with clear decision support for leadership. A standardized valuation methodology—grounded in economic, technical, regulatory, and operational factors—is needed to support investment, sharing, auction participation, relocation cost analysis, and long term spectrum strategy.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: 18 CEG (A&AS) - Engineering Support Services - Resolicit
opps.description:
This is a non-personal services task order to provide A&AS Engineering Support Services. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over the contract services providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government. 18 CEG requires assistance to provide highly reliable, highly credible professionals, with specialized technical expertise to support the 718 CES Engineering, Installation Management, and 18 CES Operations Flight. Contractor will provide planning, programming, project management, environmental support, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, civil engineering, structural engineering, architectural and inspection support on site to 718 CES/CEN including CEN's GOJ section (718 CES/CENPJ), 718 CES/CEI, and 18 CES/CEO Flights. Primary performance location will be Kadena Air Base, Japan.

Note: Market research conducted in form of MRAS with RFI #'s: RFI1797922, RFI1797923, RFI1797924, RFI1797925, RFI1797926

This is the re-solicitation of RFP1809875, originally posted on 6 May 2026. The purpose of this re-solicitation is to correct the previously published RFP close date of 22 July 2026. This solicitation will close on 25 June 2026 at 1000 hours JST (24 June 2026 at 2300 hours EST).All feedback received during the prior solicitation's Q&A session has been incorporated into this re-solicitation. The previous RFP1809875 Q&A document is also attached for your reference.

15 June 2026 Amendment 1: This amendment removes Travel CLIN 2006.4 from Attachment 2 Schedule Pricing Sheet (Excel), adds DFARS clause 252.232-7007, Limitation of Government's Obligation, and revises the proposal submission deadline to 26 June 2026 at 1000 hours JST (25 June 2026 at 2300 hours EST).

USSOCOM has a requirement for Program Management Office Support Services (PMOSS) in support of USSOCOM J6.  This requirement provides management, planning, and financial support that helps USSOCOM oversee programs, resources, budgets, contracts, and organizational initiatives. The contractor assists government leadership with planning future requirements, tracking performance, managing documentation, monitoring contractor services, coordinating working groups and meetings, analyzing data, supporting budget execution, and helping ensure programs are carried out efficiently and in accordance with government policies and objectives. The contract serves as a support function that enables informed decision-making, effective resource management, and successful execution of USSOCOM priorities.

opps.title: Notification of Intent to Award a Sole Source Purchase Order to LexisNexis
opps.description:
This notice is being published in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Subpart 5.101 requiring dissemination of information for proposed contract actions. This is a notice of intent to award a sole source purchase order. THIS NOTICE IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE PROPOSALS.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) intends to negotiate and award a sole-source purchase order to LexisNexis for StateNet. The Government intends to procure this requirement in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Part 12, specifically RFO FAR 12.102(a), for acquisitions valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT).

The period of performance for this requirement is a Base year and four (4) 1-year Option years.

The CFTC requires a tool to track state legislation concerning prediction markets with real-time alerts and forecasting capabilities. StateNet provides comprehensive coverage of legislation, regulations, and policy activity across all 50 states, Congress, and key U.S. territories. StateNet provides access to bills, rules, statutes, executive orders, legislative hearings, regulatory notices, and rulemaking activity. The CFTC is unaware of any alternative solutions that can meet the agency’s requirements.

This notice is not a competitive request for proposals.  A competitive solicitation will not be issued. Interested parties who believe they can provide these services may submit a capabilities statement to the Contracting Officer no later than July 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM Eastern Time. Responses must be submitted via email. Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of Market Research. A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed contract based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

The Government will not be responsible for any costs of preparation in response to this announcement.

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.
opps.summary:
Background
 The U.S. Census Bureau is the Nation’s primary source of statistics about the U.S. population, housing, and economy. The Census Bureau's mission includes the reliable collection, preparation, tabulation, and dissemination of demographic and economic statistics. The Decennial Census is mandated by Article I, Section 2 of the U.S. Constitution to count everyone once every ten years. 

The 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS) aims to enhance the existing systems for recruiting and managing temporary employees for the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2

opps.title: Maintaining Momentum in More Than a Job States
opps.description:
This Request for Quotation (RFQ) is prepared in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8.4 and General Service Administration Regulation (GSAR) 538.71, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice.  Proposed pricing must be offered against the contractor's current schedule contract.

FNA is seeking a contractor to provide expert technical assistance to States to expand and improve their SNAP E&T programs. The period of performance is not to exceed a 12-month base period from the date of contract award. The Contractor shall accomplish the following over the period of performance:
-	Deliver program and policy related technical assistance to up to fifteen (15) States that have previously engaged or are planning to engage with FNA to develop a More Than a Job Campaign. The purpose of the technical assistance is to strengthen State SNAP E&T programs, increase their effectiveness, and address policy and program challenges that hinder successful implementation of the More Than a Job campaign. 
-	Develop and disseminate the SNAP E&T Review, a periodic digest of SNAP E&T relevant events, announcements and other news relevant to SNAP E&T.

opps.title: Digital Asset Management System for WeRemember.ABMC.gov and the Burial and Memorialization Electronic Directory
opps.description:
American Battle Monuments Commission (ABMC) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) for market research purposes. ABMC seeks industry input on cloud based digital asset management platforms and transition approaches that can enhance or replace the current platform at WeRemember.ABMC.gov and support long term stewardship of burial and memorialization records, digital assets, and public access services.

ABMC is particularly interested in digital asset management platforms that are proven, support federal requirements, and can be configured without excessive custom development such as (in alphabetical order): Alfresco, Aprimo, ArchivesSpace, AtoM, Axiell DAMS powered by Piction, Bynder, Canto, Hyland, Nuxeo, OpenText, Preservica, ResourceSpace, and comparable digital asset management or archival platforms

See the attached RFI for full details. Please submit your responses to Jessica Young at youngj.ctr@abmc.gov by Friday, July 10, 2026.

Note that this RFI is different from the RFI titled "Burial and Memorialization Electronic Directory (BMeD) Commercial Off-the-Shelf Platforms" (Notice ID: ABMCFY27_BMeD_COTS_RFI).

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This notice has been updated to incorporate the answers to questions.

opps.title: Logistics Support Services for ACL OHIC National Training Meetings
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description: The purpose of the two annual OHIC national training meetings is to assist, support, and inform the SHIP, SMP, and MIPPA grantees nationwide. The meetings will offer an opportunity to review core values and practices that define the programs and hone new skills to help strengthen program grantees. OHIC's goal is to provide grantees with opportunities to network, learn, and explore new ways to enhance their programs. The agenda of each meeting is developed to meet the needs of these programs. Each year of the contract the New Director Training will be held in the District of Columbia metro area. The National Training Conference will rotate the venue location, to the extent possible, to a different U.S. city (to be determined by ACL) to lessen the travel burden on attendees. When holding the meetings in-person is not feasible due to travel limitations and/or grantee safety concerns, the meetings will be held virtually as necessary.

opps.title: Freshworks Licenses, Implementation, and Maintenance
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with part 12. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. Offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

Solicitation number 337372 is issued as a request for quotation (RFQ) for a consolidated, cloud-based Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) Freshworks enterprise service management (ESM) platform, or equal, to replace its existing ITSM solution and implement standardized customer and enterprise case management capabilities across the agency. 

The solution must support approximately 550 internal users and designated agents, public media and historical service inquiries, and enable consistent intake, routing, Service Level Agreement (SLA) management, reporting, automation, and role-based access control. The platform must integrate with Microsoft 365 E5 and Microsoft Entra ID, support secure public-facing intake where required, and provide enterprise-grade auditability and data governance. 

This requirement is issued on a brand name, or equal, basis for the Freshworks platform. ABMC has determined that limiting competition is appropriate and the supporting rationale is documented and maintained in the contract file.. See attached Performance Work Statement for complete details.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by
reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Questions are due no later than end of day, Tuesday, June 23, 2026. Questions submitted after this date may be considered; however, the Government does not guarantee a timely response.

Quotes are due no later than end of day, Friday, July 10

opps.title: Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC)
opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.
