This is a non-personal services task order to provide A&AS Engineering Support Services. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over the contract services providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government. 18 CEG requires assistance to provide highly reliable, highly credible professionals, with specialized technical expertise to support the 718 CES Engineering, Installation Management, and Housing Maintenance Flights as well as 18 CES Operations Flight. opps.title: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Case Management System (CMS)
opps.description: The DHA Office of Equal Employment and Resolution Management (OEERM) office requires this automated tracking system to ensure accurate management and oversight of EEO Complaints for the Agency or the production of the annual 462 report in accordance with regulatory compliance. The goal of this requirement is to continue to maintain this commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) product (Case Management Development Platform powered by Entellitrak) in order to provide a system capable of ensuring the agency is within regulatory compliance. Similar products will be implemented for other capabilities such as Anti-Harassment (AH). This COTS solution will continue to be hosted on Military Cloud (milCloud) development, test and production environments. Additional responsibility for this effort will be to provide operations, sustainment and engineering support as well as system updates and maintenance of all non-production environments.

opps.title: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access planform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components.
opps.description: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access platform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components. The Contractor shall perform research of repair line-item costs in each Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) code areas across the country to provide a database that represents the local economy in a disaster location. Services specified under this contract require quarterly updates and additional pricing work that reflects changes in prices due to current economic conditions in each FIPS code location. Develop and update the FEMA Line-Item Database in a Microsoft Access format with an interactive front-end application capable of entering a disaster number, selecting a state or territory, selecting counties, municipalities or parishes within the state or territory, and exporting the pricing data in FEMAâ€™s designated tab delimited format described in Attachment B. 3.1.1. The line-item format includes a category, line-item number, line-item name, unit of measure and a description of repair represented by the line item. The line-item cost is a sum of all cost components associated in the use of that line item. The database shall be updated quarterly, delivered to FEMA through an FTP (File Transfer Protocol) site to be imported by FEMA. The first deliverable of the base and each option year shall be delivered in the first week of: March=Q1, June=Q2, September=Q3, December=Q4. Develop an annual Line-Item Mapping Master â€“ A Microsoft Excel file of the Line-Item basis and rationale used for pricing that provides a breakdown of the line-item components as CSI codes with national average costs.

opps.title: NIJ-NamUs (National Missing and Unidentified Persons System)
opps.description:
NOTE 1: RFQ submission due date is MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET.
Submit all quotations by MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 at 12:00PM ET.
GSA eBuy system stated close date of Wednesday, July 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET is INCORRECT.
NOTE 2: ---RFQ1814958 (100% small business set-aside) HAS BEEN CANCELED-- 
RFQ1815768 (unrestricted) REPLACES RFQ 1814958--- Submit quotations to RFQ1815768 only.

National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ). The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to:
(1) operate without interruption,
(2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and
(3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.

The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.

Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor. The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them. Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institute of Justice (NIJ), part of the U.S. Department of Justice's Office of Justice Programs (OJP), is seeking to establish a contract for the National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NamUs) program. This initiative aims to assist federal, state, local, and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners, coroners, allied professionals, and the general public in resolving cases of missing persons, unidentified individuals, and unclaimed persons.

The NamUs program is crucial for providing national assistance in identifying victims and supporting criminal investig

This oversight must be supported by advanced technology and expertise. The intent of instituting governance, disciple and a viable operational environment will enhance customer experience and CBPâ€™s overall mission while potentially reducing time and costs.

Disclaimer
THIS IS FOR INFORMATION PURPOSES ONLY.  This notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes it does not constitute a Request for Quotes (RFQs) or Request for Proposals (RFPs), nor does this information commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Further, the Government is not seeking quotes/proposals at this time and will not accept unsolicited proposals. The Government will not entertain questions pertaining to the announcement now, nor any future acquisition for Space Systems Command's (SSC's) requirements. The Government will provide updated information once available and will be posted on GSA eBuy for industry awareness.

Overview
The United States Space Force (USSF) Space Systems Command, Enterprise Sourcing Division (SSC/PKA) has released three (3) Request For Quotes (RFQs) of the six (6) projected Commercial SSSV Constellation Blanket Purchase Agreements (BPAs): Administrative Support, Cybersecurity Support, and Acquisition Support. SSC/PKA projects to release the remaining three (3) Commercial solicitation during calendar year 2026 (CY2026) for: Financial Management Support, Engineering Support, and Security Support.  All RFQs will be posted on GSA eBuy only, and a Special Notices For Information Purpose Only regarding the posted solicitations on eBuy to each respective BPA will be posted on SAM.gov to inform industry of potential teaming or subcontract opportunities and encourage Teaming or Subcontract Arrangements with General Services Administration (GSA) Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) Holders.

The requirement encompasses the delivery of brand-name or equal equipment that meets or exceeds all technical specifications identified in the Statement of Work (SOW).

- Laptops and desktop computers

- External PIV card readers

All equipment must comply with applicable CISA security, performance, and compatibility standards and must integrate seamlessly with the Agencyâ€™s existing IT environment.

opps.title: Oil Blending Simulation Software and Support
opps.description: Software and Support for Oil Blending Simulation - It will reduce the cost for generating a new crude assay ($25K-$35K) when/if the formulation of a given blend has changed. For this software to be fully functional will require the one-time purchase of at least one reference assay library and training on software. There will be annual fees for license support, assay library updates, and 2nd and 3rd year payments on the HCAMS license fee. If/When we end restrictions on accepting only domestic crudes for storage we will require a more robust assay library to properly evaluate blending properties of oils using foreign blend stocks.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.description: In support to Goal 4 of the NOAA Strategic Plan, which addresses support to national Commerce and Transportation needs, the Marine Chart Division (MCD) manages (i) the national database of nautical charting and marine mapping information, (ii) the aggregation of information from external sources for database maintenance, (iii) the production of navigational products and services, and (iv) the distribution of digital chart products. The MCD directs the development of new nautical products and promotes, maintains communication with users, manages personnel and resources, fosters efficiency in operations to provide effective products and services to the marine public, and develops and authorizes changes in charting standards and specifications to meet national and international marine safety and transportation requirements. opps.title: Weather Stream MicroWave Sounder Data Buy
opps.description: This acquisition seeks access to acquire commercial, near-real-time MWS data, including optional hyperspectral observations, from on-orbit MWS systems. The focus is on Low Earth Orbit (LEO) observations suitable for operational data assimilation, short-term forecast improvement, tropical cyclone monitoring, precipitation-related applications, and environmental hazard assessment. Depending on sensor design, MWS may also provide imagery and atmospheric vertical wind profiles. The capabilities described in Section II.B and II.C  will provide NOAA with scalable and flexible access to commercial MW data, enhancing forecast accuracy, situational awareness, and NOAA’s role as the Nation’s authoritative source of environmental intelligence.

opps.title: FISMA Assessment Services
opps.description: The requirement is for a contractor to conduct a full security and privacy control assessment, covering 100% of the controls for a Moderate / Moderate / Moderate FISMA system that has a High Value Asset overlay. The successful vendor must demonstrate the necessary skills and in-depth knowledge to ensure the assessment fully complies with federal mandates, including DOC and NOAA policies, and NIST requirements (such as NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2 and NIST SP 800-53A Rev 5). The primary goal is to produce accurate assessment findings that support an Authority to Operate (ATO) recommendation.1
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
No direct capability lane match found — scope may not align with BLN24 proven delivery
⚠️ Scope gap: Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

pp_refs: IRS Human Centered Design & Research BPA ($7M)
Dept of Education EDARMST Data Analytics ($2.9M)
tasks.note: BLN24 prime — consulting/advisory embedded in major contracts (IRS, Census, Education)

opps.title: Pitchbook Platform and Direct Data Feed Subscription
opps.description: The purpose of this Order is to procure a subscription to Pitchbook Data, Inc.'s business intelligence data ("Pitchbook Platform") and the Pitchbook Direct Data Feed. Accordingly, 1. This is a firm-fixed price Order for nine (9) standard seats plus one (1) direct data feed seat for DOE for a one-year base subscription period of 9/29/2023 - 9/28/2024, one option year (9/29/2024 - 9/28/2025) that may be exercised at the sole discretion of the Government. 2. The ARPA-E portion is $24,142 and the portion of the other DOE offices is $91,800 for an aggregate firm fixed price amount of $115,942. Additionally, a Contract Line Item is added for Other Direct Costs in the not-to-exceed amount of $17,142. 3. The following attachments are incorporated into this Order: Attachment A - Pitchbook Renewal Agreement Attachment B - Pitchbook Subscription Agreement Attachment C - Pitchbook Direct Data Feed Agreement 4. In the event of a conflict between the terms and conditions of the Pitchbook agreements and the U.S. Government
win_reasons: Partial data/analytics scope match — verify fit vs Census/Education delivery experience
Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*
win_reasons: Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside
OUT OF SCOPE: ship — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Scope gap: DevSecOps — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

tasks.note: BLN24 prime — Census SRQA, MAF/TIGER, EDL all heavy data engineering
Adjacent (2/4) — Census SRQA used agile + DevSecOps principles; IRS Forms Mod used CI/CD pipelines. Not standalone DevSecOps prime but confirmed delivery within broader contracts.
BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Majority of task areas require teaming — may not be best lead

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$50050.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

opps.title: Cyber Security Program Support
opps.description: NOS requires comprehensive support to ensure organizational compliance with Department of Commerce (DOC), NOAA, and FISMA policies by providing technical and non-technical cybersecurity assistance and consultation. The system's primary objective is to manage the NOS cybersecurity posture through the continuous identification of sustainable business process requirements, oversight of security incidents through the NOAA Incident Response and Reporting Application (NIRRA), and management of all organizational data calls. Vendors are expected to provide qualified labor to maintain enterprise-level dashboards, validate user permissions for operational tools such as Tenable Security Center, and provide expert guidance based on NIST Special Publications to improve IT operations and reduce risk. Furthermore, the system may require optional technical assistance for specific FISMA system boundaries, including maintaining core security documentati

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: Integrating Technology into the Delivery of Clinical Care at Health Centers
opps.description:
The purpose of this initiative is to advance the integration of technology, digital innovation (through AI and patient wearable devices) to support more efficient chronic disease prevention and control, with a focus on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, across HRSA-funded health centers and to reduce health center administrative burden. 
Wearable devices, including smartwatches, rings, fitness trackers, and continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), generate continuous patient health data that, when securely integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems, equip providers with timely, actionable insights to improve clinical decision-making, close gaps in care, and support value-based reimbursement. 
This contract will also incorporate sister agency initiatives where applicable, including CMS' ACCESS and FDA's TEMPO programs, and will complement the five pillars of the HHS AI Strategy.
By improving digital and health literacy for both patients and providers, this initiative centers on a patient-focused approach; empowering patients, strengthening shared decision-making, and aligning with HHS's Making America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative. Through tailored technical assistance, peer learning, and evaluation, this program will help health centers implement strategies that measurably improve outcomes for patients and communities across the country.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) is initiating a contract to advance the integration of technology and digital innovation in clinical care at health centers. The goal is to enhance chronic disease prevention and management, focusing on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, while reducing administrative burdens. 

This initiative aims to empower patients and improve health outcomes through the use of AI and wearable devices that generate patient health data, which can be integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems for better clinical decision-making.

Background
 The U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC) through the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) is initiating the Public Wireless Supply Chain Innovation Fund (PWSCIF), a $1.5 billion discretionary grant program aimed at accelerating the commercial development of open-interface, standards-based, compatible, interoperable equipment.

opps.title: Strategic Communications Services for the National Assessment Governing Board - RFQ 91995926Q0010
opps.description:
*06/17/2026 - Amendment A0001 - Please review the document 91995926Q0010-A0001-Q-A for the Department’s responses to questions received by the clarification questions due date, and spreadsheet uploaded to this notice. The due date for quotes is unchanged.*

**PLEASE READ THIS NOTICE AND ALL ATTACHMENTS IN ITS ENTIRETY**

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial services, issued in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Part 12.201-1 – Simplified Procedures. This announcement serves as the only solicitation. Please follow all submission instructions carefully.

This is a total small business set-aside under NAICS Code 541820 – Public Relations Agencies – Small Business Size Standard is $19,000,000. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov.

Solicitation number 91995926Q0010 is issued as a request for quotations (RFQ) for Strategic Communications Services for the National Assessment Governing Board. The Governing Board seeks communications experts with a demonstrated knowledge of education policy and first-hand expertise working with media in the education sector. The work shall entail creating a broad communications strategy and supporting its implementation. This implementation includes developing content, drafting messages, articles, and posts for both traditional and social media, identifying and prioritizing audiences for outreach and partnership building, and ascertaining the best tactics and channels to communicate Natioanal Assessment of Educational Progress results and the Governing Board’s work, such as Board policies and actions. See the Statement of Work - Attachment A -  for specific requirements. 

The Department will only accept written clarification questions from potential quoters via email. Clarification questions must be submitted no later than 12:00 pm ET on Wednesday, June 1

The purpose of this acquisition is to obtain non-personal commercial services to provide the NGB Staff Support Services support to the National Guard Bureau divisions/directorates. Within CISA, the Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) executes critical responsibilities related to planning, coordinating, and sustaining the Agencyâ€™s engagement with councils, advisory bodies, committees, and other formal governance structures that support national-level critical infrastructure security and resilience.  To execute these functions effectively, SED requires contractor support to plan, manage, operationalize, document, and sustain the full lifecycle of council and advisory activities, as well as to facilitate cross-council coordination, strategic communication, and records and governance management essential to CISAâ€™s broader responsibilities. The forthcoming contract will provide support across:

Â·        Strategic and Operational Engagement Support: Planning, coordination, logistics, documentation, and execution of classified and unclassified engagements.

opps.title: Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III. Historically, EGIS has served as the enterprise integration backbone connecting USCIS internal systems with Department of Homeland Security (DHS), interagency, and external partners. While this core mission remains critical, EGIS will also expand this role supporting national security, immigration system integrity and advanced adjudication operations with key mission including: National security vetting and evolving immigration policies; screening immigration benefit applicants; protecting immigration benefits and work authorization integrity; advanced data integration, analytics, AI-enabled anomaly detection, and decision support; agility, rapid delivery, operational excellence, transparency, and reduced duplication; fraud detection, risk scoring, and investigative data workflows. 
As of 3/3/2026 we are in the market research/ acquisition planning phase of this acquisition, as such, some of the APFS fields may change over the coming months. This record will continually be updated once changes to the strategy are known.

opps.title: USACE UMSB Army Vantage Integration Field Service Representative (FRS)
opps.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, USACE Mission Support Battalion (UMSB) is executing a directed modernization initiative to transition operational workflows from manual, locally managed processes to structured, enterprise-integrated systems aligned with Army Vantage and the Microsoft ecosystem. This effort supports federal and Army digital transformation mandates requiring measurable progress in data consolidation, automated reporting, and enterprise visibility. One (1) senior-level Field Service Representative (FSR) will need to be embedded at Humphreys Engineer Center, Alexandria, Virginia, with periodic presence at a secondary Government location in Washington, DC beginning in Month 4. The FSR shall perform duties consistent with the approved Performance Work Statement (PWS) across five task areas: backend integration support, workflow documentation and transition support, user training and adoption support, integration status reporting, and stakeholder engagement and coordination. The FSR shall provide on-site technical and change management support, working directly with workflow owners, UMSB leadership, and the UMSB Operations (OPS) office. The overarching program objective for the base period is to achieve the HR Integration End State as defined in Section 3 of the Performance Work Statement (PWS), establishing a replicable integration model for future directorate-level transitions. Leadership's primary operational objective is to utilize Army Vantage's built-in templated dashboard functions, populated from authoritative HR data sources, as the standard method for HR data visibility and decision support, replacing manual data pulls and locally managed reporting.

The announcement does not constitute a formal Request for Quote (RFQ) or Proposal (RFP) and is not an indication that the DOC will contract for any of the items and/or services presented in this document and attachments. Any formal solicitation that may subsequently be issued will be announced separately. Information on the specific topics of interest is provided in the following sections of this announcement. The DOC is not responsible for any cost incurred by responders to this RFI. After a review of responses, the Government may at its discretion contact one, more than one, all or none of the respondents to obtain additional information necessary to determine Respondent capabilities and/or to obtain clarification on information provided.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

competitors.why: Premier federal evaluation firm. Wins IES and ED evaluation contracts regularly. They are THE main competitor on any IES program evaluation work — they essentially invented rigorous federal program evaluation. Check their IES-specific awards on HigherGov.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source - Bloomberg Government Subscription Service
opps.agency: DHS Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Bloomberg Industry Group, Inc, 1801 South Bell Street Arlington, VA 22202.

 Background

In support of the S&T mission, many divisions need access to certain scientific journals and databases to remain at the forefront of their research.  Bloomberg Government is a premier platform for federal policy, budget, and legislative intelligence. The proposed purchase will enable DHS S&T staff to access real-time government data, track legislative developments, and conduct policy analysis, thereby enhancing operational effectiveness and strategic decision-making. The acquisition is expected to streamline research, improve situational awareness, and support mission-critical activities.  S&T will be procure five  (5) subscriptions for (5) users.

Bloomberg Government (BGOV) is a unique, proprietary platform that provides comprehensive, real-time federal legislative, regulatory, and budgetary intelligence specifically tailored for government affairs professionals. No other commercially available product offers the same breadth and depth of integrated data, advanced analytics, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards in a single solution. BGOV’s exclusive aggregation of congressional activity, appropriations tracking, policy analysis, and federal contract intelligence is essential for DHS S&T’s mission to monitor and respond to rapidly evolving legislative and regulatory developments. After conducting market research and evaluating alternative so

opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation and request for quotes (RFQ) for commercial products or commercial services in accordance with the procedures of Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) part 12 - Acquisition of Commercial Items and FAR part 13 - Simplified Acquisition Procedures, with FAR part 12 taking precedence per FAR 13.500(b)’s reference to FAR 12.102(c).

The FHFA requires online access to a commercially available off-the-shelf (COTS) credit research, analysis, and relevant data subscription from a Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organization (NRSRO) approved by the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Please see the attached documentation for additional details.

opps.title: SECURITY PROCESSING SUPPORT SERVICES
opps.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
opps.description:
The objective of this BPA is to provide diverse technical personnel security, adjudicative and administrative support services for suitability-based background investigations and corresponding support for related special projects, coordination of background check investigations of potential sponsors, case management, coordination with federal and state child protective services, and the creation and issuance of Personal Identity Verification (PIV) badges and their affiliated credentials to the ISBS customer base of approximately 10,000 federal employees and contractors. Accordingly, ISBS processes upwards of 25,000 individual cases per year for ISBS traditional lines of business in background investigations and badging, and upwards of 30,000 individual cases per year for ISBS lines of business in Child Abuse and Neglect Checks and fingerprint checks, as well similar support for special projects.
HHS is seeking a contractor to provide a wide range support. The associated general titles and roles are described in the Project Roles section herein. The listing of roles is not exhaustive, but the duties identified are inclusive of the minimum level of work currently required by HHS

opps.title: R408--Radiation Oncology Assessment Services
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF WORK




	GENERAL INFORMATION



	Title of Project: Radiation Oncology Services Assessment Services for National Radiation Oncology Program, (NROP) 11SPEC22.


2. Scope of Work:  The essential services to be performed by a subject matter expert with knowledge and experience with VHA National Radiation Oncology Program. The contractor will perform the following tasks:

2.1. Review and Recommendations:

 Comparison of non-VA radiation oncology facility practices with NROP Office established performance guidelines and providing recommendations for approvals, and coordination of approval notifications to community practices.

2.2. Coordination of Chart Reviews:

The contractor shall provide specialized coordination and administrative clinical support to facilitate physician Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluations (OPPE) and focused chart review activities for radiation oncology sites that operate with limited physician staffing, including single-physician facilities.  The services are essential to ensure continuity of care, regulatory compliance, and maintenance of high-reliability clinical operations in highly complex radiotherapy environments.   

2.3. Data Management:

 Perform complex, short-term projects for NROP to evaluate credentialing data reports and to provide some development assistance for NROP to manage data for purposes of reporting at micro and macro levels.

2.4. Conference Coordination:

Organize and manage logistics for conferences hosted by the National Radiation Oncology Program, including conference approvals, contacting speakers, and handling pre- and post-conference relevant tasks.   

2.5 Conduct Surveys:

Conduct customer satisfaction surveys in the field as required by SCPO and the NROP.


	Background: The National Radiation Oncology Program provides clinical oversite for the 41 VHA radiation oncology services/sections, setting policies for radiation therapy delivery and organizing and developing current infrastructure.


Since
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Radiation Oncology Program (NROP) provides clinical oversight for the 41 VHA radiation oncology services/sections, setting policies for radiation therapy delivery and developing current infrastructure. 

Since 2020, there has been a significant increase in NROP initiatives, including quality surveillance and legislative proposals. The administrative integrity of the program requires assessment services to ensure best practices for ongoing needs.

opps.title: Executive Training focusing on Communication
opps.description:
REQUIREMENT: Executive Leadership, Communication, & AI Integration Training
SET-ASIDE: 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside (FAR 8.405-5)
GSA SIN: 611430 (Professional and Management Development Training)

DESCRIPTION:
The Department of War (DoW) requires specialized, instructor-led training for executive-level personnel (supervisors and above). Standard, entry-level professional development courses will not be accepted.

The contractor shall provide customized training covering the following five (5) specific modules:

Module	Topic Focus
1	Communication Techniques
2      Communication Style
3	Adaptability and Change Management
4	Managing Difficult Conversations
5	Utilizing AI Tools with Confidence

KEY CONSTRAINT: To accommodate executive schedules, each individual training module shall NOT exceed 2.5 hours in length.

INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS:
This requirement is being competed as a 100% Small Business Set-Aside. Only quotes from verified Small Business GSA Schedule holders under SIN 611430 will be evaluated.

Please review the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS) for full technical requirements and the Evaluation Criteria document for submission instructions. Quotes must be submitted as two distinct volumes (Technical and Price) exactly as outlined in the evaluation attachments.

opps.description:
The DHS Office of Privacy requires contractor support to analyze, standardize, and transform over 4,500+ legacy privacy compliance artifacts (including Privacy Threshold Analysis, Privacy Impact Assessment, and related documentation) into a modernized Privacy Risk Framework. The office is implementing a new standardized template framework aligned with the Privacy Act, E-Government Act of 2002, OMB guidance, and the NIST Privacy Framework. This effort is not limited to document conversion.
The contractor shall:

â€¢	Map content to the Privacy Risk Framework domains and scoring criteria
â€¢	Identify privacy risks, mitigations, and control gaps
â€¢	Enable future system ingestion and automation
â€¢	Support development of repeatable, scalable workflows
â€¢	Provide decision-support outputs for leadership

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products and commercial services as prepared in accordance with the format in Revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.212(b)(1), as supplemented in the attached Request for Quotation (RFQ). This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. A firm-fixed price purchase order is anticipated.  RFO 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services will be used. This solicitation is unrestricted. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov. 
The associated North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code for this procurement is 512110, with a small business size standard of $40 Million. 
The VA Puget Sound Healthcare System is seeking to purchase Whole Healthcare Channel Subscriptions Brand Name or Equal to the C.A.R.E. Channel for their Tacoma, WA and Seattle, WA locations.
Service Locations:

Option Year Two:     08/01/2028   07/31/2029
Option Year Three:   08/01/2029   07/31/2030

Offers must be emailed to Jennifer Robles jennifer.robles1@va.gov and received no later than 1:00 PM Mountain on 07/09/2026. 
Questions to this solicitation shall be submitted via electronic mail to the Contracting Officer Jennifer Robles (jennifer.robles1@va.gov). Question

opps.title: Electronic Security Consulting Services
opps.description:
Provide services in accordance with the attached Statement of Work. The primary goal of establishing this contract is for electronic security consulting services to support the procurement and design phases of the U.S. Marshals Service (USMS) electronic security system project for Courthouses in Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN.

Please read the attached documents for information on the scope of work and additional information included. The consulting services outlined focus on supporting GSA through scope development, design review, procurement, and initial security contractor onboarding for new electronic security systems (Access Control, Video Surveillance, Intrusion Detection, and Intercom/Emergency Communication). Services will primarily be performed remotely, with necessary on-site activities at Washington, D.C., Atlanta, GA, Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN. The estimated duration for these services is 2-years from the commencement date, with implementation support projected from August 3, 2026, to August 3, 2028.

The Government intends to award an order for services in accordance with FAR 8.4 (GSA Class Deviation RFO-2025-08) on the basis of best value to the Government. All evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are approximately equal to price. 

The technical factors that will be evaluated are the offerors Past Experience and Key Personnel, and will be of equal weight when evaluating the proposals.

Past Experience

The Offeror shall demonstrate past experience designing, installing, integrating, testing, and commissioning electronic security systems for facilities of similar size, scope, and complexity. Relevant systems include access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection, duress alarms, intercom systems, alarm monitoring, and related security infrastructure.

The Offeror shall submit two project examples completed within the past five years. These examples shall involve federal facilities or courthouses. Project exampl

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following:

opps.title: OneStream Implementation for Financial Management
opps.description:
To to obtain professional services for system configuration, production deployment, and ongoing operational support of the financial management components of the USCIS OCFO OneStream system, which is based on the commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) OneStream software.
USCIS OCFO has acquired OneStream on a SaaS basis and currently utilizes it for budget formulation and execution, with planned expansion into people planning and strategic alignment. The system is hosted by OneStream, and USCIS holds sufficient software licenses.
Throughout the task order period, the Contractor will employ USCIS Agile development methodology and follow OIT IT system development best practices. The Contractor will collaborate with OCFO business subject matter experts and product owners to gather requirements, configure the system, and deploy capabilities/modules incrementally via Agile sprints. Upon deployment to the production environment hosted in the OneStream FEDRAMP-authorized vendor cloud the Contractor will provide operations and maintenance (O&M) support for the system components.
Implementation will be managed in accordance with industry standards and USCIS-provided software development project management tools. The Contractor must demonstrate expertise in OneStream software capabilities, architecture, and security parameters, and transfer this knowledge to federal staff through informal knowledge transfer for users both within and outside OCFO.
Upon completion of this task order, USCIS OCFO will have operationalized system components that are adopted and used by OCFO accountants and Financial Management Division (FMD) Financial Operating Center (FOC) staff, comply with security requirements and federal financial management regulations, and interface effectively with USCIS, DHS, and other federal systems to support efficient business operations.  See attached PWS for details.

This was previously posted on SAM.gov, under the same RFI number. 

Please also email responses to david.p

opps.title: Geospatial Support Services
opps.description:
The United States Department of Justice (DOJ), Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), is requesting information and conducting research by process of a Market Survey to identify qualified vendors who possess the capabilities to provide support services that directly supports the FBI's Geospatial Support Services for the Directorate of Intelligence within the Analysis Technology Unit (ATU). The information gathered may be used to formulate acquisition strategies for competitive solicitations. This Market Survey is open to *large and small businesses that's an active participant with  GSA MAS Schedule*. Responses will be reviewed and maintained by the United States Federal Government. 

*Contemplated Requirements-See Attachment 01*

Market Survey Questions: (Also Attached)

1.	Can you support multi-machine ESRI ArcGIS Enterprise Deployments within the Azure government cloud and disconnect Cloud on Premise environment? Explain your experience.
2.	What is your experience working with FBI or other Federal Agency IT Security Standards within controlled environments to obtain and maintain an Authority to Operate( ATO)?
3.	Describe your ability to staff positions in Huntsville, AL and Washington, DC with TS/SCI clearance, along with your strategy to retain/fill vacancies as needed within a reasonable amount of time. 
4.	What is your ability to provide continuous technical and geospatial related training and professional development opportunities for your onsite staff?
5.	Describe your organization's current ESRI Partner network tier (Platinum, Gold, Member, Startup) and outline the benefits you receive as an official ESRI partner at that level. If you are not an ESRI partner, explain your ability to team or subcontract to a vendor that holds an ESRI partnership status.



As permitted by FAR 10, this is a market research tool being utilized for informational and planning purposes ONLY. Your responses will assist the Government in the development of its acquisition strategy

- Multi-machine E

opps.title: JB,MDL SONET Ring Replacement
opps.description:
The purpose of this Request for Quote is for the acquisition of replace a SONET Ring at Eight (8) each locations, to include SRS to the appropriate GFE CAT 5e, or Thin Net COAX, or Fiber Optic Patch Panels (FOPP) at each location with the applicable Contractor EFI&T patch cords. All FOPP terminations are Snap-and-Twist (ST) connectors. At all locations, cabinet rack space is available for the Contractor to EFI&T mount their equipment. Each cabinet has spare 110VAC, 60Hz recessed female outlets for the Contractor to EFI&T their SRS's power connections or the required Contractor EFI&T UPS. The Contractor shall EFI&T patch cables from these cabinets to the official GFE Demarcation Point, within the serving Comm Room(s). The Government shall provide cut sheets 10 days after contractor award, detailing circuit terminations for each location and the respective patch panels. The Government
requires a commercial, carrier grade SONET/optical transport solution compatible with existing JB MDL network infrastructure throughout Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst (JBMDL), New Jersey, in support of the 87 Communication Squadron (87 CS). This requirement is being solicited in accordance with FAR Part 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services.

See Attch 1 - Statement of Work, for Specific requirements.

opps.title: Global Distribution System (Reservation System)
opps.description:
The TSA business process of providing a global distribution system (GDS) to schedule, make hotel reservation and secure flight itineraries/tickets through a direct connectivity reservation system, is a mission critical process.   It is imperative this reservation system be US-based because of the strict security requirements.   TSA has direct accountability to provide efficient and effective bookings on commercial carriers, with the ability to select seating in order to address in a fluid and timely (up to the minute) manner, all threats, new and old.  The computer software reservation system must provide a full range of travel services necessary to support the government at multiple facilities.

The anticipate period of performance is for a base one-year period, with four (4) one-year periods.

opps.title: OER - NOAA Ship Telepresence (FY27 - FY30)
opps.description: This license will provide video/imagery telepresence support for OER Ocean Expeditions on NOAA Ship Okeanos Explorer, and replacement NOAA Ship Discoverer and other platforms/projects; satellite upload / download ship to shore of large data volumes and video streams, with critical live communication to/from shore subject matter experts, universities, cooperative institutes, and outside agencies to expand exploration and understanding of the ocean environment, and allow for immediate awareness and distribution of findings to the ocean community at-large. While video is archived at NOAA NCEI, there is no comparable viewable portal to access the video for the ocean community at large or to reach global subject matter experts to identify findings. This mechanism serves as the publicly viewable access point for video and images that are acquired on the NOAA Ship and through other OER funded projects. Annotated video data from other platforms

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

competitors.evidence: IES, ED, HHS — program evaluation, RCT design, education research

opps.title: Electrical Law Library (ELL)
opps.description:
This is a pre-solicitation notice of a proposed contract action issued in accordance with part 12 – Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This is not a solicitation or invitation for offerors to submit bids.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons (FBOP), located at 320 First St. NW, Washington DC, anticipates issuance of request for quotation (RFQ) 15BPCC26Q00000020 for a single award firm-fixed price (FFP) definitive contract for electronic legal research to be utilized all Bureau of Prisons institutions throughout the United States.

The performance period will include a one-year base followed by one option period with an Option to Extend Services FAR 52.217-8.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This will be solicited open market on an unrestricted basis. The NAICS code for this requirement is 519290 – Web Search Portals and All Other Information Services and PSC Code R605 – Library Services.

A solicitation will be issued on or about March 19, 2026, and will be posted to SAM.gov, with an estimated closing date of March 26, 2026. Hard copies of the solicitation will not be made available.  The website provides download instructions.  Any secure/locked documents (if applicable) uploaded with the solicitation will require an active Sam.gov vendor registration for access.  If you encounter problems accessing these documents, please contact the Federal Service Desk at 866-606-8220.  All future information about this solicitation, including any amendments, will also be distributed solely through this website.  Interested parties are responsible for continuously monitoring this website to ensure that they have the most recent information about this proposed contract action.

IMPORTANT: Any contractor interested in obtaining a contract award with the Federal Bureau of Prisons must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov. All offerors are urged to double check their SAM registration

opps.title: Maintenance of ISO 9001: Certification Audits
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial services prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes only the solicitation; proposals are being requested, and a written solicitation will NOT be issued. 

This is a Request for Quote (RFQ) using FAR Part 12, Acquisition of Commercial Services; the Solicitation Number is W519TC-26-Q-ISO2.

The solicitation and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through the FAR. 

The NAICS code for this procurement is 541611; the small business standard is $25,000,000.00. The Product Service Code is R704.

Offerors must be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) database at time of response to this solicitation. The website for SAM is https://www.sam.gov/
Addendum to 52.212-1.

DESCRIPTION OF THE REQUIREMENT

This solicitation is issued for Maintenance of ISO 9001: Certification Audits at Crane Army Ammunition Activity (CAAA), in accordance with the Performance Work Statement as Attachment 0001. 

The Contract Line Item Number (CLIN) Structure is as follows:
CLIN 0001 Stage Audit 1 (S1)
CLIN 0002 Stage Audit 2 (S2)
CLIN 0003 Travel Year 1 Audits
CLIN 0004 Surveillance Audit 3 (SA3)
CLIN 0005 Surveillance Audit 4 (SA4)
CLIN 0006 Travel Year 2 Audits
CLIN  0007 Surveillance Audit 5 (SA5)
CLIN 0008 Recertification Audit (RC1)
CLIN 0009 Travel Year 3 Audits

TYPE OF CONTRACT, PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE and PERFORMANCE LOCATION

This acquisition is issued as a 100% Small Business Set-A-Side. Award will be made on a firm-fixed price basis for CLINs 0001, 0002, 0004, 0005, 0007, and 0008. CLINs 0003, 0006, and 0009 (Travel Expenses) will be awarded on a cost-reimbursable not-to-exceed basis.

The Period of Performance is as follows:
The Period of Performance is FY 2026 FY 2028.
Fiscal Year 2026: 1 Oct 2026 - 30 Sep 2027
Fiscal Year 2027: 1 Oct 2027 - 30 Sep 2028
Fiscal Year 2028: 1 Oct 2028 - 30 Se

Leadership and team development services

-Past performance 
- Price

USGS is tax exempt.

opps.title: Request for Terminal Instrument Procedures (TERPS)
opps.description:
The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency's (NGA) Source Foundation Aeronautical Navigation Office (SFA) has a worldwide requirement to provide time sensitive Safety of Navigation (SoN) Aeronautical information and data to support operational needs of the Department of Defense (DoW). The Government's objective is to identify and select a commercial provider to develop Terminal Instrument Procedures (TERPS) production software that delivers the optimal user experience and become a trusted SFA industry partner essential to SFA's daily production of Aero Foundation GEOINT data. Aeronautical data content management includes compilation, validation, and maintenance of worldwide aeronautical navigational data, products, and services for integration into the Foundation GEOINT content.

Questions are due by May 11th at 5pm CST.

opps.title: Web-Based Application Tax Research Tool for the Treasury Office of Tax Policy
opps.description:
Sources Sought – Web-Based Application Tax Research Tool for the Treasury Office of Tax Policy

RFI Number: 2032H326N00008

Notice Type: Sources Sought

GENERAL PURPOSE

1.0 Purpose 

The Department of Treasury’s Office of Tax Policy creates and maintains tax policies and programs, continuously evaluates regulations and rulings for the Internal Revenue Code, negotiates tax treaties, and provides economic and legal policy analysis for domestic and international tax policy decisions. To achieve their deliverables, Tax Policy requires a web-based subscription to a comprehensive tax research tool.


	
	
		Acquisition Approach
	
	


The Treasury has not determined the acquisition approach. Any results of this Sources Sought will assist the Treasury Departmental Offices and the Departmental Offices & Enterprise Procurement Branch, Treasury Common Services Center – Acquisition Services (TCSC) in determining an acquisition strategy about how to proceed.

SOURCES SOUGHT REQUIREMENTS

2.0 Description of Information Requested

TCSC Acquisition Services is requesting information regarding the respondents’ capabilities in providing the aforementioned required.


	Provide company information, to include:
	
		Company name and address;
		Size of company (number of personnel, total revenue, ownership, parent corporation, etc.);
		Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
		Company point(s) of contact including name, phone number, address, and email; length of time in business;
		Geographic presence;
		Company experience in providing valuation services to government or commercial entities; 
		Provide any pertinent information that will assist us in this assessment.
		Contract Number and value/capacity; and
		Available Strategic Contracts (e.g., GSA, Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract allowing decentralized ordering, etc.). Please identify type of “ordering” contract (e.g., GSA Multiple Award Schedule, GSA Professional Service Schedule
win_reasons: Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage
